CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT SANDBOX SUITES, INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT JOIE DE VIVRE HOSPITALITY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION KARR, DONALD MOES 1 TO 50

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BARBARY INSURANCE BROKERAGE AND GERALD BECERRA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ALLEGATIONS SUPPORTING THE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY ARE INDISTINGUISHABLE FROM THOSE SUPPORTING THE CLAIM FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE. GIVEN THE LATTER, THERE IS NO NEED FOR THE FORMER. MOREOVER, NO CALIFORNIA AUTHORITY SUPPORTS A BREACH OF FIDUCIARY CLAIM UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. DEFENDANT TO FILE AN ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR # 11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT DONALD KARR'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. GIVEN THE EVIDENCE CONCERNING THE LIKELIHOOD OF LIABILITY, THE LIKELY ALLOCATION OF ANY LIABILITY, AND THE ABSENCE OF COLLUSION, THE SETTLING PARTIES HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS WITHIN THE BALLPARK UNDER THE TECH-BILT FACTORS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Mtn For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ory sandel, esq. and richard idell, esq., 415.986.2400, for plaintiffs nieto fine art san francisco, llc et al.
thomas minder, esq., 916.929.4400, for defendant sandbox suites.
kelly kerr, esq., 510.457.9925, for cross defendant donald kerr.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT DONALD KARR's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is Denied. Given the evidence concerning the likelihood of liability, the likely allocation of any liability, and the absence of collusion, the settling parties have not shown that the settlement is within the ballpark under the Tech-Bilt factors. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD J. IDELL OF IDELL & SEITEL LLP, TEL. NO. 986-2400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARGARET PORTO & ATTY. ANDREW G. WANGER OF CLYDE & COM LLP, TEL. NO. 365-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS BARBARY INSURANCE BROKERAGE AND GERALD BECERRA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Sustained without leave to amend as to third cause of action. The allegations supporting the claim for breach of fiduciary duty are indistinguishable from those supporting the claim for professional negligence. Given the latter, there is no need for the former. Moreover, no California authority supports a breach of fiduciary claim under these circumstances. Defendant to file an answer within 10 days. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR # 11918 =(302/HEK)
